qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated OAuth client registration exhaustion denies new client onboarding
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a loopback /register capacity-exhaustion issue in packages/server/src/index.ts and packages/server/src/oauth.ts, not the advisory's missing scoped-workspace enforcement in packages/mcp/src/tools/knowledge.ts and packages/mcp/src/tool-scoping.ts.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause: scoped-agent access to knowledge_search or knowledge_get_node with caller-controlled cross-workspace reads because authContext-based workspace scoping is missing.
evidence: The advisory describes a broken access control flaw in packages/mcp/src/tool-scoping.ts and packages/mcp/src/tools/knowledge.ts where scoped agents can invoke knowledge_search or knowledge_get_node and supply an arbitrary workspaceId or node target without authContext-enforced workspace restriction.

The only final qualifying finding, oauth_client_registration_exhaustion, targets unauthenticated POST /register handling in packages/server/src/index.ts and the 100-entry in-memory client table in packages/server/src/oauth.ts.

That finding's demonstrated consequence is temporary denial of new OAuth client registrations, which does not share the advisory's vulnerable handlers, authorization bypass mechanism, or cross-workspace knowledge disclosure path.
